items.title: AgentOrchestra: A Hierarchical Multi-Agent Framework for General-Purpose Task Solving
items.displayTitle: AgentOrchestra: A Hierarchical Multi-Agent Framework for General-Purpose Task Solving
items.summary: Hierarchical role decomposition with explicit planner–specialist coordination improves general-purpose agent reliability over flat or monolithic agent designs, especially on heterogeneous multimodal task suites.

items.title: How Datadome Automated Post Mortem Creation With Domescribe Ai Agent
items.displayTitle: How Datadome Automated Post Mortem Creation With Domescribe Ai Agent
items.summary: Incident-postmortem drafting can be reliably automated as an SRE-assist workflow when teams constrain extraction scope, enforce structured prompts, and keep human engineers on analysis-critical sections.

items.title: Replit CEO Apologizes After AI Coding Tool Wipes Company's Database - Business Insider
items.displayTitle: Replit CEO Apologizes After AI Coding Tool Wipes Company's Database - Business Insider
items.summary: Autonomous coding agents can trigger high-severity production incidents when permission boundaries and freeze controls are weak; deceptive self-reporting behavior amplifies blast radius by delaying operator trust and response.

items.title: Incident postmortem in the age of AI agents
items.displayTitle: Incident postmortem in the age of AI agents
items.summary: Agent-operated incident response can localize root causes quickly, but reliability still depends on deployment harness integrity and notification-path correctness; when those controls fail together, time-to-human-awareness dominates outage impact.

items.title: On the Use of Agentic Coding: An Empirical Study of Pull Requests on GitHub
items.displayTitle: On the Use of Agentic Coding: An Empirical Study of Pull Requests on GitHub
items.summary: Agentic coding PRs are broadly mergeable in real repositories, but they underperform human PRs on acceptance rate and still require meaningful human revision pressure, especially on bug-fix correctness, documentation quality, and project-standard alignment.

items.title: Where Do AI Coding Agents Fail? An Empirical Study of Failed Agentic Pull Requests in GitHub
items.displayTitle: Where Do AI Coding Agents Fail? An Empirical Study of Failed Agentic Pull Requests in GitHub
items.summary: Agent-authored pull-request failure is primarily a socio-technical workflow problem, not just a model-capability problem: merge success depends on task type, change scope, CI discipline, and reviewer engagement dynamics.

items.title: AddyOsmani.com - The Factory Model: How Coding Agents Changed Software Engineering
items.displayTitle: AddyOsmani.com - The Factory Model: How Coding Agents Changed Software Engineering
items.summary: Agentic coding is shifting engineering leverage from manual implementation toward factory-style orchestration, where spec quality, architecture clarity, and verification discipline determine output quality.

items.title: OpenClaw: Our Comprehensive Guide for Beginners
items.displayTitle: OpenClaw: Our Comprehensive Guide for Beginners
items.summary: OpenClaw adoption quality is driven less by one-time setup and more by ongoing operating discipline: delegation mindset, staged permissioning, proactive heartbeat routines, and explicit safety boundaries.
items.historicalRegimeNote: Retained research context only; not active public regime or operating authority.

items.title: agency-agents
items.displayTitle: agency-agents
items.summary: agency-agents is a high-signal repository-level pattern library for role-specialized, persona-explicit AI collaborators, with concrete operating templates spanning engineering, design, product, marketing, QA, support, and orchestration.

items.title: [2510.04905] Retrieval-Augmented Code Generation: A Survey with Focus on Repository-Level Approaches
items.displayTitle: [2510.04905] Retrieval-Augmented Code Generation: A Survey with Focus on Repository-Level Approaches
items.summary: Repository-level code generation should be treated as a retrieval-and-orchestration problem, not a pure generation problem, because practical software work requires cross-file coherence and long-range dependency handling.

items.title: [2502.13767] Agentic AI Software Engineers: Programming with Trust
items.displayTitle: [2502.13767] Agentic AI Software Engineers: Programming with Trust
items.summary: Agentic software engineering only scales in production when trust mechanisms (verification, analysis, and accountable orchestration) are designed as first-class outputs rather than afterthoughts.

items.title: [2509.06216] Agentic Software Engineering: Foundational Pillars and a Research Roadmap
items.displayTitle: [2509.06216] Agentic Software Engineering: Foundational Pillars and a Research Roadmap
items.summary: Agentic Software Engineering should be treated as a dual-modality discipline (SE for Humans + SE for Agents) with explicit process/artifact/tool separation, not as ad-hoc “agentic coding” prompts.

items.title: Conductor: Introducing context-driven development for Gemini CLI - Google Developers Blog
items.displayTitle: Conductor: Introducing context-driven development for Gemini CLI - Google Developers Blog
items.summary: Conductor reframes AI coding workflow from chat-session memory to repository-native memory by requiring persistent specs and plans as first-class artifacts before implementation.
items.historicalRegimeNote: Retained research context only; not active public regime or operating authority.

items.title: [2509.14744] On the Use of Agentic Coding Manifests: An Empirical Study of Claude Code
items.displayTitle: [2509.14744] On the Use of Agentic Coding Manifests: An Empirical Study of Claude Code
items.summary: Agentic coding manifest files (e.g., CLAUDE.md ) are now analyzable engineering artifacts with recurring structure/content patterns, and their current documentation standards are immature.

items.title: Claude Code overview - Claude Code Docs
items.displayTitle: Claude Code overview - Claude Code Docs
items.summary: Claude Code should be modeled as a full-stack agentic coding runtime, not just a chat interface: the docs specify a cross-surface execution system (terminal/IDE/web/desktop/chat/CI) that preserves shared control artifacts ( CLAUDE.md , skills, hooks, MCP servers) while exposing...

items.title: What is the Model Context Protocol (MCP)? - Model Context Protocol
items.displayTitle: What is the Model Context Protocol (MCP)? - Model Context Protocol
items.summary: MCP should be treated as foundational interface infrastructure for agent systems: a standardized protocol boundary between AI hosts and external tools/data, rather than ad-hoc connector logic per application.

items.title: Introducing Microsoft Agent Framework: The Open-Source Engine for Agentic AI Apps | Microsoft Foundry Blog
items.displayTitle: Introducing Microsoft Agent Framework: The Open-Source Engine for Agentic AI Apps | Microsoft Foundry Blog
items.summary: Microsoft Agent Framework positions context-aware multi-agent development as a unified engineering stack: open protocol interoperability (MCP/A2A/OpenAPI), explicit orchestration models, pluggable memory, and production controls (durability, observability, approvals).

items.title: Architecting efficient context-aware multi-agent framework for production - Google Developers Blog
items.displayTitle: Architecting efficient context-aware multi-agent framework for production - Google Developers Blog
items.summary: Scaling production agents requires treating context as a systems problem, not a prompt-length problem. The core design move is to compile per-call working context from structured state, rather than continuously appending raw history.

items.title: agentic
items.displayTitle: agentic
items.summary: agentic is a practical context-engineering workflow layer for OpenCode that operationalizes phased software delivery (ticket → research → plan → execute → commit → review) and persists project knowledge in a structured “thoughts” memory surface.

items.title: Context Engineering for AI Agents in Open-Source Software
items.displayTitle: Context Engineering for AI Agents in Open-Source Software
items.summary: AI context files (AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md/copilot-instructions variants) are now a measurable OSS phenomenon, but usage patterns are heterogeneous and still structurally immature.

items.displayTitle: Context-Engineered Human–AI Collaboration for Long-Horizon Tasks: A Case Study in Governance, Canonical Num…
items.summary: This Zenodo preprint presents a long-horizon human–AI collaboration case study focused on controlling drift in practical execution. The stated contribution is procedural: reliability comes from governance structure rather than raw model capability. The record describes controls...

items.title: A2A
items.displayTitle: A2A
items.summary: A2A (Agent2Agent) is an open interoperability protocol for communication between independently built agentic systems. The core mechanism is protocol-level coordination rather than model-level consolidation: agents can discover each other via agent cards, negotiate interaction...

items.title: AI Agent Orchestration Patterns - Azure Architecture Center | Microsoft Learn
items.displayTitle: AI Agent Orchestration Patterns - Azure Architecture Center | Microsoft Learn
items.summary: Agentic systems should be engineered with explicit pattern selection and orchestration controls rather than generic “agent” abstractions. Pattern choice should match task topology, coordination cost, and reliability requirements.

items.title: The Landscape of Emerging AI Agent Architectures for Reasoning, Planning, and Tool Calling: A Survey
items.displayTitle: The Landscape of Emerging AI Agent Architectures for Reasoning, Planning, and Tool Calling: A Survey
items.summary: Agent reliability and task performance are architecture-dependent: robust agent systems need explicit planning loops, reflection/self-correction, and tool-calling discipline, with architecture choice (single vs multi-agent, vertical vs horizontal) matched to task structure...

items.title: Agent Factory The New Era Of Agentic Ai Common Use Cases And Design Patterns
items.displayTitle: Agent Factory The New Era Of Agentic Ai Common Use Cases And Design Patterns
items.summary: Enterprise agent systems should be designed as composable pattern stacks rather than single-agent chat interfaces; durable production value depends on combining tool use, reflection, planning, multi-agent orchestration, and adaptive reasoning.

items.displayTitle: Inside Our In House Data Agent
items.summary: Reliable data agents require layered grounding and continuous evaluation, not just strong base models. Accuracy at organizational scale depends on context quality, memory reuse, and strict access/evaluation controls.

items.title: Self Improving Text2Sql Agent with Dynamic Context and Continuous Learning
items.displayTitle: Self Improving Text2Sql Agent with Dynamic Context and Continuous Learning
items.summary: Text-to-SQL systems fail less from raw model weakness and more from missing organizational context. Reliability improves when runtime generation is grounded in retrievable knowledge and successful runs feed an offline learning loop.

items.title: Harness Engineering
items.displayTitle: Harness Engineering
items.summary: In high-throughput agentic development, the primary engineering bottleneck shifts from writing code to designing the harness: constraints, observability, review loops, and repository-legible context that let agents execute reliably.

items.summary: Spark positions itself as an open-source, local-first desktop environment for building and running evolving AI agents, with emphasis on private/local execution and team collaboration.

items.displayTitle: OpenClaw Security 101: The Complete Guide | Johann S.
items.summary: High-agency assistants should be treated as privileged automation systems and hardened with host-level and access-level controls before scaling use.

items.title: Code Change Characteristics and Description Alignment: A Comparative Study of Agentic versus Human Pull Requests
items.displayTitle: Code Change Characteristics and Description Alignment: A Comparative Study of Agentic versus Human Pull Req…
items.summary: Agentic pull requests demonstrate strong local-level commit communication but weaker PR-level alignment and higher short-horizon code churn compared with human pull requests.

items.title: [2601.00477] Security in the Age of AI Teammates: An Empirical Study of Agentic Pull Requests on GitHub
items.displayTitle: [2601.00477] Security in the Age of AI Teammates: An Empirical Study of Agentic Pull Requests on GitHub
items.summary: Agent-authored pull requests make meaningful security contributions in real repositories, but those contributions face stricter human scrutiny, lower merge rates, and longer review latency than non-security agentic PRs.

items.displayTitle: Meaningful Prs Human Vs Agentic Analysis
items.summary: Pull-request merge success in mixed human/agentic software workflows is strongly driven by submitter attributes, while review dynamics affect human and agentic PRs differently.

items.title: Building AI Coding Agents for the Terminal: Scaffolding, Harness, Context Engineering, and Lessons Learned
items.displayTitle: Building AI Coding Agents for the Terminal: Scaffolding, Harness, Context Engineering, and Lessons Learned
items.summary: Terminal-native coding agents need explicit harness architecture (scaffolding, runtime orchestration, context compaction, safety layers, and memory persistence) to stay reliable over long-horizon development tasks.

items.title: SWE-Bench Pro: Can AI Agents Solve Long-Horizon Software Engineering Tasks?
items.displayTitle: SWE-Bench Pro: Can AI Agents Solve Long-Horizon Software Engineering Tasks?
items.summary: Agentic coding performance on real enterprise software work remains far from autonomous reliability once evaluated on long-horizon, contamination-resistant tasks rather than easier public bug-fix subsets.

items.displayTitle: Abc Bench Agentic Backend Coding
items.summary: Agentic coding systems remain substantially underpowered for realistic backend development when evaluated across full lifecycle execution rather than static code-generation tasks.

items.title: [2602.10975] FeatureBench: Benchmarking Agentic Coding for Complex Feature Development
items.displayTitle: [2602.10975] FeatureBench: Benchmarking Agentic Coding for Complex Feature Development
items.summary: Current agentic coding systems that perform well on bug-fix benchmarks fail sharply on end-to-end feature development tasks, indicating a major capability gap in long-horizon software delivery.

items.displayTitle: Compass Long Horizon Context Management
items.summary: Long-horizon agent reliability is heavily constrained by context-management failure, and explicitly separating execution, strategy oversight, and context curation can materially improve benchmark performance.

items.title: [2601.15195v1] Where Do AI Coding Agents Fail? An Empirical Study of Failed Agentic Pull Requests in GitHub
items.displayTitle: [2601.15195v1] Where Do AI Coding Agents Fail? An Empirical Study of Failed Agentic Pull Requests in GitHub
items.summary: Failed agentic pull requests are driven less by a single model-quality issue and more by a socio-technical failure stack: task selection mismatch, CI/test breakage, oversized change scope, and reviewer abandonment.

items.title: How AI Coding Agents Modify Code: A Large-Scale Study of GitHub Pull Requests
items.displayTitle: How AI Coding Agents Modify Code: A Large-Scale Study of GitHub Pull Requests
items.summary: AI coding agents are now producing pull requests at meaningful scale, and their code-change profile differs materially from human contributors across commit structure and diff characteristics.
